nuggets.headline: Imagine leaving a major record label and then hitting a creative wall so hard, you almost break up. That was Radiohead's reality before 'In Rainbows'.
nuggets.text: After fulfilling their contract with EMI, Radiohead embarked on creating their seventh album in 2005 with a desire to shake things up, initially attempting to record without their long-time producer Nigel Godrich. These early sessions proved 'frustrating and fruitless,' with Thom Yorke admitting they spent a long time 'just not going anywhere'. It was only after touring with embryonic versions of the songs and re-enlisting Godrich—who, according to Yorke, gave them a 'walloping kick up the arse'—that the band found its direction and regained confidence, leading to the groundbreaking self-release of *In Rainbows* with its 'pay-what-you-want' model.

nuggets.headline: The mesmerising 'underwater' feel of 'Weird Fishes/Arpeggi' isn't just atmospheric; it's a deliberate rhythmic puzzle crafted by the band.
nuggets.text: The song's distinctive texture comes from its intricate, layered arpeggios, particularly the guitar parts played in groups of 3/8 notes, which intentionally create a rhythmic dissonance against the steady 4/4 drum beat. This polyrhythmic complexity, combined with the poetic lyrics by Thom Yorke that explore themes of hopelessness and escape by likening the mind to a deep ocean, truly immerses the listener in a unique sonic world. Notably, an early orchestral version of 'Arpeggi' was performed in 2005 with the London Sinfonietta, featuring Jonny Greenwood on the Ondes Martenot.

source.title: xsnoize.com
source.quoteSnippet: The arpeggios in Weird Fishes are played from high to low in groups of 3/8 notes, creating rhythmic dissonance with the drum part.

nuggets.headline: If the layered, ethereal soundscapes and intricate rhythms of 'Weird Fishes/Arpeggi' resonated with you, then you absolutely need to dive into Grizzly Bear's album, 'Veckatimest'.
nuggets.text: Grizzly Bear's 2009 album, *Veckatimest*, offers a similar tapestry of lush, atmospheric soundscapes and complex arrangements that echo Radiohead's craftsmanship on *In Rainbows*. Their music shares a delicate balance between intricate instrumentation, often featuring interwoven vocal harmonies, and a powerful emotional resonance that makes for a truly immersive listening experience, much like the track you just heard. Some fans even describe it as a 'heavier In Rainbows' due to its rich, expansive sound.

source.title: xsnoize.com
source.quoteSnippet: Their album Veckatimest is kinda like a heavier In Rainbows in some ways. Just as full of beautiful, lush, spacious soundscapes but with a little more “oomph” to it.

nuggets.imageCaption: Grizzly Bear 'Veckatimest' — lush harmonies meet intricate arrangements
